workflow.name: SDLC Parallel Assessors Mixed Outcomes
workflow.goal: Ship a `workflow_cli release-audit --scenario-name <name>` command where revision 1 adds the baseline audit output, quality requests changes for missing rollback and compatibility release-readiness details, revision 2 resolves those quality findings but still omits the permission-boundary hardening, and revision 3 resolves the remaining security findings so stale earlier approvals do not clear the newest revision.

parameters.initial_revision_scope: Revision 1 MUST add `python3 -m workflow_cli release-audit --scenario-name <name>` that emits deterministic JSON with `scenario_name`, `audit_status` set to `draft`, `rollback_ready` set to `false`, and an empty `compatibility_notes` array. Revision 1 MUST NOT add a non-empty rollback checklist, README usage, `permission_boundary`, `threat_model_note`, or `guardrail_validation` yet.
parameters.quality_rework_scope: Revision 2 MUST update `release-audit` to emit a non-empty `rollback_checklist`, `rollback_ready=true`, and a non-empty `compatibility_notes` array. Revision 2 MUST also document the command in `README.md` and extend unit coverage for the release-ready quality fields. Revision 2 MUST NOT yet add `permission_boundary`, `threat_model_note`, or `guardrail_validation`.
parameters.security_rework_scope: Revision 3 MUST add the remaining security-only fields to the `release-audit` payload: non-empty `permission_boundary`, non-empty `threat_model_note`, and `guardrail_validation=true`. Revision 3 MUST extend unit coverage so the active subject revision fully verifies those security-only fields.
parameters.mixed_outcome_contract: The quality assessor MUST request_changes on revision 1 because the active subject still lacks the rollback checklist, compatibility notes, and README usage. The security assessor MUST approve revision 1, reject revision 2 while the security-only fields are still missing, and approve revision 3 after the subject revision increases and fully satisfies `security_rework_scope`.

instructions.content: This scenario uses an authored three-revision contract for the concrete `workflow_cli release-audit` command. Revision 1 MUST stop at `initial_revision_scope`. Revision 2 MUST satisfy `quality_rework_scope`. Revision 2 MUST NOT satisfy `security_rework_scope`. Revision 3 MUST satisfy the full `security_rework_scope` after the security rejection is routed back through the architect. Treat simulator-style implementations, precomputed future revisions, or early delivery of later-scope items as contract noncompliance, not as helpfulness.
